root_motivation: Today's flat MEMORY.md store has no decay signal, no duplicate detection, and no link integrity check, so stale or contradictory notes silently mislead future sessions. Before building retrieval, embeddings, or push-mode, prove the file layout is healthy: a deterministic linter that walks the Markdown memory store and reports stale, duplicate, and broken-link entries gives the user (and Claude) a trusted hygiene baseline to iterate retrieval against. This V1 slice does not retrieve, rank, or write memories; it only audits the on-disk corpus so later phases stand on a clean foundation.
user_persona: Single user (jsy) on one laptop running Claude Code, plus the Claude sessions that read/write ~/.claude/recall/. Linter is invoked manually from a shell or by a scheduled hook; output is consumed by humans reading the terminal and by tooling parsing JSON.

acceptance_criteria.description: CLI binary `recall-lint` accepts a positional path to a memory root directory and exits 0 when the directory is empty, emitting an empty findings array in JSON mode.
Walks the root recursively, treats every *.md file as a memory entry, and ignores non-Markdown files and dot-prefixed directories.

Parses YAML frontmatter (optional, fenced by ---). Recognizes `last_recalled_at` and `created_at` as RFC3339 timestamps; flags entries whose newest of (last_recalled_at, created_at, file mtime) is older than --stale-days (default 30) as finding kind=`stale`.
Detects duplicates: two entries with byte-identical body (post-frontmatter, trimmed) produce one finding kind=`duplicate` listing all paths in deterministic sorted order.

Detects broken `[[wiki-links]]`: a link `[[target]]` resolves if any *.md file under the root has stem `target` or relative path `target.md`; otherwise emits finding kind=`broken_link` with source path, target, and 1-indexed line number.

Output mode is selectable: `--format text` (default, human-readable, grouped by kind) or `--format json` (stable schema with top-level `findings` array; each finding has `kind`, `paths`, and kind-specific fields). JSON output is deterministic given fixed input.
Exit code reflects findings: 0 = no findings, 1 = at least one finding, 2 = invocation error (bad path, unreadable file, malformed args). Exit codes are stable across runs on identical inputs.
Lints a fixture corpus of 100 *.md files in under 500ms wall time on a warm cache (single-thread). Verified by a `#[ignore]`-gated bench-style test that asserts elapsed < 500ms.
Frontmatter is optional; entries without frontmatter use file mtime as the freshness signal and are never flagged as `stale` solely because frontmatter is absent.
Supports `--ignore <glob>` (repeatable) to skip paths matching the glob; ignored files contribute neither to duplicate detection nor to wiki-link resolution.

scope: Rust 2024 CLI crate `recall-memory-linter` producing a `recall-lint` binary.
Recursive walk of a memory-root directory; treat *.md files as entries.
YAML frontmatter parsing for `last_recalled_at` and `created_at` fields only.
Three lint kinds: stale (age threshold), duplicate (body-identical), broken_link (unresolved [[wiki-link]]).
Two output formats: human-readable text and stable JSON.
Deterministic output ordering (sorted paths, sorted findings by kind then path).
Exit codes mapping (0/1/2) suitable for shell pipelines and pre-commit hooks.
Unit and acceptance tests using fixture corpora committed under tests/fixtures/.
non_goals: No retrieval, ranking, or top-k query API.
No embeddings, vector index, FTS5, or SQLite anywhere.
No reading from or writing to ~/.claude/recall/ at runtime; path is user-supplied.
No memory writes, edits, or auto-fix; linter is read-only.
No daemon, no Unix socket, no MCP server, no Claude Code hook integration.
No PostToolUse observation, no SessionStart injection, no Stop-hook promotion.
No episodic/semantic/procedural/reflective typing — all *.md files are treated uniformly.
No confidence scoring, no recall_count tracking, no supersedes chain handling.
No within-session scratch handling, no compaction-survival logic.
No cross-project boosting or project-tagging logic.
No network calls of any kind; no telemetry.
No GUI, no TUI, no web UI; CLI only.
Not a general-purpose Markdown linter — only the three kinds listed in scope.

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a memory linter as the V1 slice of recall, rather than retrieval or embeddings?
five_whys_trace.a: The PRD names stale, duplicate, and unaudited memories as the proximate harm — 'a note saved on 2026-01-15 has the same weight today as a note from yesterday' and 'stale memories actively mislead me.' Retrieval that surfaces bad data is worse than no retrieval, so hygiene must come first.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must hygiene come before retrieval rather than in parallel?
five_whys_trace.a: Retrieval ranking is impossible to evaluate without a clean ground-truth corpus. If the store contains duplicates and broken links, ranking metrics measure noise. The linter produces the corpus signal that downstream phases need.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a CLI linter specifically, instead of a library called from the future daemon?
five_whys_trace.a: A CLI has the smallest surface area that still delivers value today: the user can run it against the existing ~/.claude flat-Markdown store in week 1, get a hygiene report, and act on it. A library with no callers is dead code; a CLI is immediately exercised, and the same logic can later be lifted into a library.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the output be deterministic and exit-coded?
five_whys_trace.a: Determinism makes the linter testable under autobuilder's iterate-and-prove loop (golden fixtures), and stable exit codes let the user wire it into pre-commit or scheduled hooks without parsing text. Both properties are load-bearing for the iteration harness to compare runs.

five_whys_trace.q: Why these three lint kinds (stale, duplicate, broken_link) and not more?
five_whys_trace.a: These are the three failure modes the PRD names explicitly as currently-broken (sections 1.4, 7-architecture wiki-link layout, and the implicit duplicate-risk in a flat namespace). Anything else (confidence calibration, evidence-excerpt verification, supersedes-chain validation) requires schemas the V1 file layout has not yet committed to, so linting them now would over-fit a guess.

ambiguities_resolved.question: User was not available for live 5-Whys; what is the V1 carve from a multi-phase PRD?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Inferred from PRD Phase 0/1 ('Migrate existing memory layout, no behavior change' and 'File store + keyword index') and from risk 'Memory becomes a leash' — the smallest valuable slice is a read-only hygiene auditor. Deferred everything in phases 2-5 to non_goals.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the linter consume ~/.claude/recall/ directly, or accept an arbitrary path?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Arbitrary path. Avoids tight coupling to a layout the PRD itself calls 'open question 1' (separate repo vs ~/.claude/), keeps tests hermetic via fixtures, and lets the user dry-run against any directory.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What counts as 'stale'? PRD mentions 30d as decays_after example but it is a per-memory field, not a global default.
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Default --stale-days=30, configurable. Linter uses max(frontmatter.last_recalled_at, frontmatter.created_at, file mtime) as the freshness signal. If frontmatter omits both timestamps, file mtime alone is used and absence-of-frontmatter alone is not a stale signal.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD shows a JSON memory schema with id/kind/subject/etc. Should the linter validate that schema?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. V1 file layout has not been committed (PRD section 7 is illustrative, not normative). Schema validation is deferred to a future phase once the layout is canonical.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the canonical wiki-link syntax — [[name]], [[name.md]], [[path/to/name]]?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Accept [[target]] where target is matched against (a) any *.md file's stem under root, or (b) relative path target.md from root. Both forms resolve; anything else is a broken link.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Harness command for the unfakeable metric — should it be cargo test count or a custom binary?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Use cargo test exit-status counted via grep on 'test ... ok' lines. Total tests passing is the single load-bearing scalar; iterate-and-prove maximizes it. Future phases can swap in latency/binary-size when those become load-bearing.
